hard.next_step: Small update as I am in Struer today and have talked to finance. As finance are swamped in the year end activities June/May, we have pushed next step to later in June.
hard.competitor: ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation (2 unnamed peer finalists); incumbents SAP ECC/Bear Shop, SharePoint, Adobe Sign; future Microsoft Dynamics ERP path

north_star_verdict.headline: Strong AI-led CLM/P2P evaluation stalled at the commercial step: Anthony sent the phased Phase-1 proposal on 14 May, Jonas said he would 'review and revert', and it has gone quiet for ~7 weeks while B&O finance is buried in year-end close. Jonas (now Head of Technology) is building the board business case but cannot submit it until the numbers are agreed and finance is pulled in.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Oct 2025; Shortlisted since 9 Dec 2025 (~6.9 months in stage as of 6 Jul 2026). Close 30 Nov 2026 = 147 days out. Last activity 22 Jun 2026 (14 days ago) was a rep-side re-engagement to-do, not a buyer touch; last genuine incoming buyer email 15 May 2026 (52 days ago, Jonas acknowledging the phased proposal). Amount was cut $138k->$70k on 8 May 2026 into a phased modular roadmap. Pace to close by 30 Nov: agree commercials and quantify ROI now, board recommendation/decision Aug-Sep, contract Sep-Oct, implementation Q4 - achievable only if the commercial thread restarts within two weeks and finance (Mikkel Staun Joergensen) is engaged ahead of the IT & Digital Board.

evidence.point: Anthony Gray delivered the phased Phase-1 S2P commercial proposal on 14 May 2026 and Jonas Elber replied 15 May 'I will review and revert if any questions' - no substantive reply since, as finance is swamped with year-end close (next step pushed to later June on 16 Jun 2026).

Deal scope narrowed: Amount cut from $138k to $70k on 8 May 2026 and the proposal restructured as 'a clean, modular roadmap - Phase 1 covering...' - a defensive, phased posture, not a full-suite commitment.
evidence.source: SF OpportunityFieldHistory 2026-05-08 (Casper Hoeholt); Clari email 2026-05-14 (Anthony Gray)

evidence.point: Jonas Elber, promoted to Head of Technology, owns the CLM/S2P recommendation and rated Zycus ahead of competition on AI on 25 Nov 2025 ('you have come quite far on it compared to competition... you are in a good position') - a strong active champion building the board business case.
evidence.source: Avoma CLM demo part 2 2025-11-25 (Jonas Elber); SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-05-29

evidence.point: Best Case forecast on a $70k deal with the IT & Digital Board / CFO never engaged, no booked next meeting, the original May 2026 go-live already missed, and scope cut - the category is not defensible.

metrics.narrative: The pain is clear but has never been quantified. On 29 Oct 2025 Jonas Elber said most purchase orders arrive as free text, 'so it's difficult to me, as responsible for the spend, to see what are we spending our money on', and on 20 Nov 2025 framed P2P as a broken hand-off because 'procurement owns this process but we have owners in finance who owns the PTP process and we don't talk to each other'. The CLM driver is roughly 10,000 contracts sitting in SharePoint that are 'difficult to manage' (20 Nov 2025). SF Business_Objectives lists compliance, risk and automation but attaches no cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure, and no ROI model exists. With scope now cut to a $70k phased Phase 1 and two unnamed S2P peers who may undercut on price, quantifying spend leakage from free-text orders, contract-cycle time and legal review hours is the open move so the board decides on value, not price.

economic_buyer.narrative: The buying authority is mapped but never engaged. Jonas Elber stated on 25 Nov 2025 that the recommendation 'does need to go to an IT and Digital Board where our CFO is involved... making the final decision of each of these projects', and the IT & Digital Board has already approved budget for the evaluation. The reachable finance path is Mikkel Staun Joergensen, Head of Treasury and Tax (a real B&O contact), whom the deal team itself flagged as the intro target for economic-buyer validation on 22 Jun 2026; Casper's 16 Jun next step ('talked to finance... finance are swamped in the year end activities') confirms finance now gates the timing. The CFO has never been named or briefed, and no finance stakeholder has appeared on a call. The move is to have Jonas introduce Mikkel and pre-sell the board before vendor selection.
sources: SF Task 2026-06-22 (EB-validation to-do naming Mikkel Staun Joergensen)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria surfaced clearly across the demos: AI maturity (Jonas grilled Zycus on LLM architecture, hallucination controls and multi-LLM orchestration on 25 Nov 2025 and rated us 'quite far... compared to competition'), dual-ERP support (Flemming Herold Hoejgaard on 27 Nov 2025: 'Are you aware that you need to support two ERP systems... most of our people are in SAP'), Microsoft Word and Outlook integration for legal (Kristine Green Moeller: 'I just like to work in Word'), change management (Jonas's own stated worry, 'the big problem of mine also is this change management part'), a sandbox/POC to let users feel the interface (Nickolaj Almskou Holler, 27 Nov), reference customers, and a phased/modular commercial roadmap. These are the levers the board will weigh; confirmed from calls, with price/ROI still the open variable.
sources: Avoma CLM demo part 2 2025-11-25 (Jonas Elber, Kristine Green Moeller)
Avoma P2P demo part 2 2025-11-27 (Flemming Herold Hoejgaard, Nickolaj Almskou Holler)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal, board-gated evaluation. Jonas confirmed on 17 Mar 2026 that 'the IT Transformation steerco (ITTStc) approved we continue the P2P evaluation based on 3 vendors with a S2P focus. Vendor selection end of April and recommendation/approval end of May/beg June', after which the IT & Digital Board (CFO) gives final sign-off. B&O has chosen NOT to run a full RFP for P2P - Jonas on 27 Nov 2025: 'we have come so far... that we will stick with the ones that we are discussing with unless something pops up'. Jonas owns the recommendation and drives the agenda and stakeholder list; Nickolaj (transformation) sets the timeline and gatekeeps. The internal decision timelines have repeatedly slipped, so the live process risk is a competitor reaching the board with a stronger case while our commercials are unresolved.
sources: Avoma P2P demo part 2 2025-11-27 (Jonas Elber, Nickolaj Almskou Holler)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only lightly formed. As a phased CLM-led purchase (iContract + Merlin, $70k Phase 1) this is an Order Form + SOW motion rather than a full-suite MSA, with legal owned by Kristine Green Moeller (Head of Legal Operations) and Jette Mose (Project Manager, Legal and IP). B&O signs today with Adobe Sign (Jonas, 25 Nov 2025: 'We are using Adobe Sign today') and asked whether Certinal could replace it, so e-signature is a priced option, not a blocker. No InfoSec questionnaire, DPA, or redline exchange has started, and no contract-expiration forcing function is on record. Visibility comes from agreeing the phased commercials, then confirming legal owner, InfoSec scope, and whose paper before the board decision.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Kristine Green Moeller; Jette Mose)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well articulated and owned. Jonas Elber, as spend owner, cannot see spend because most POs are free text (29 Oct 2025); the P2P process is fractured across procurement and finance who 'don't talk to each other' (20 Nov 2025); ~10,000 contracts live in SharePoint and are 'difficult to manage' (20 Nov 2025); and budget-vs-invoice control is manual, which is why Jonas pressed on budget thresholds and invoice-over-budget alarms in the 27 Nov demo. Change management is a self-identified pain, especially in legal where Kristine works manually in Word. Confirmed pain, pain owner is Jonas; what remains inferred is the financial magnitude, which is why quantification is the metrics move.

champion.narrative: Jonas Elber is a strong, active champion and has grown in weight. He owns the CLM/S2P recommendation, declined a full RFP because he is already driving it, set the demo agenda and stakeholder list, publicly rated Zycus ahead of competition on AI (25 Nov 2025), and was promoted to Head of Technology on 29 May 2026 while preparing the board business case. The gap is a second senior advocate: Nickolaj Almskou Holler (Director, Global Business Services & Transformation) requested references, pricing and a sandbox on 27 Nov 2025 but has not resurfaced on a captured call, and no finance sponsor is engaged - so the deal is single-threaded on Jonas at the exact moment it needs a finance and board bridge.
sources: Avoma P2P demo part 2 2025-11-27 (Jonas Elber, Nickolaj Almskou Holler)

competition.narrative: This is a formal ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation (Jonas, 17 Mar 2026) - Zycus plus two peer finalists B&O has not named - layered over incumbents being displaced (SAP ECC / Bear Shop free-text procurement, SharePoint contract storage, Adobe Sign) and an undecided future ERP path leaning Microsoft Dynamics. Zycus's defensible wedge is AI maturity, which Jonas rated ahead on 25 Nov 2025, plus proven dual-ERP support that de-risks the SAP-to-Dynamics migration. The live threat is a cheaper or ERP-native peer reaching the board with a stronger, quantified business case while our commercials sit unresolved and scope has been cut. Forcing a named-competitor disclosure and defending the Merlin agentic story before vendor selection is the competitive move.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We are a well-liked, AI-differentiated finalist in a 3-vendor board-gated S2P/CLM evaluation with a strong champion (Jonas, now Head of Technology) building the business case, but the deal has stalled at the commercial step for ~7 weeks, scope was cut from $138k to a $70k phased Phase 1, and the CFO/board and finance have never been engaged - value, not price, is what wins it.

win_position.text: Why this number: Shortlisted caps confidence near 70; we earn a mid-band read on a strong champion plus an AI edge Jonas rated 'ahead of competition' (25 Nov 2025), but hold below the cap because the economic buyer is unengaged and scope was cut $138k->$70k on 8 May 2026.

Jonas Elber owns the recommendation, chose a no-RFP direct evaluation, and was promoted to Head of Technology on 29 May 2026 while preparing the board business case - a champion with rising internal weight.
The scope shrink to a $70k phased Phase 1 modular roadmap signals a defensive, cost-cautious buyer and drags the win read down.
Two unnamed S2P peers remain in the ITTStc 3-vendor evaluation and could undercut on price while our ROI is unquantified and commercials are unresolved.

deal_momentum.text: The commercial thread has been silent ~7 weeks since Jonas's 15 May 'I will review and revert', with the next step pushed to later June as B&O finance is buried in year-end close.
The 22 Jun 2026 activity was a rep-side re-engagement to-do, not a buyer touch, and no next meeting is booked - momentum is rep-pushed, not buyer-pulled.
Commercial topics (phased pricing, legal, e-signature) are firmly in play, and the close date was extended to 30 Nov 2026 on 29 May, giving realistic room to recover.

customer_commitment.text: B&O committed budget for the evaluation via the IT & Digital Board and engaged five-plus stakeholders across procurement, legal, finance and architecture in the Nov 2025 demos.
Commitment is concentrated in Jonas alone; the CFO/board and finance (Mikkel Staun Joergensen) have never been engaged, so there is no confirmed budget owner behind the number.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on Jonas with no second senior advocate at the board - if he stalls, the deal stalls.
Best Case forecast is indefensible on a $70k scope-cut deal with an unengaged EB and a missed original go-live; honest category is Pipeline.
Change management is a buyer-stated worry (Jonas: 'the big problem of mine is this change management part', esp. legal), which a rival could exploit with a lighter-touch pitch.

customer_commitment.evidence: Budget approved by the IT & Digital Board but no EB/finance engaged; commitment concentrated in Jonas alone (Avoma 25 Nov 2025; SF Next_Step 16 Jun 2026).

deal_risk.evidence: Scope cut $138k->$70k on 8 May 2026, single-threaded, commercials silent ~7 weeks (SF OpportunityFieldHistory; Clari 15 May 2026).

scope_change.from: $138k full S2P + CLM evaluation (P2P suite plus iContract/Merlin)
scope_change.to: $70k phased Phase 1, modular roadmap (CLM-led iContract + Merlin)
scope_change.detail: Amount cut from $138k to $70k on 8 May 2026 and the proposal restructured by Anthony on 14 May 2026 as 'a clean, modular roadmap - Phase 1 covering...'. The buyer is getting defensive on cost and wants a phased implementation over a big-bang commitment, which lowers the win read and warrants a monitor watch.

deal_movement.summary: Movement in 2026 is commercial and defensive: scope cut to a phased Phase 1, the phased proposal delivered and acknowledged, the close date pushed to 30 Nov, then a finance-year-end stall that silenced the thread for ~7 weeks.

items.change: Amount cut from $138k to $70k, scope narrowed to a phased Phase 1
Anthony Gray delivered the phased Phase-1 S2P commercial proposal to Jonas
Jonas acknowledged the proposal: 'I will review and revert if any questions'
Close date pushed 31 Jul -> 30 Nov 2026; Jonas promoted to Head of Technology and preparing the board business case
Next step pushed to later June - B&O finance swamped with year-end close
Rep-side re-engagement to-do logged (business case + intro to Mikkel Staun Joergensen); awaiting buyer reply

competitive_position.summary: A formal ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation (Jonas, 17 Mar 2026) with two unnamed peer finalists is the strongest current threat, layered on incumbent displacement (SAP ECC/Bear Shop, SharePoint, Adobe Sign) and an undecided Dynamics-leaning ERP path. Zycus's defensible edge is AI maturity, which Jonas rated ahead of competition on 25 Nov 2025; the live risk is a cheaper or ERP-native peer reaching the board with a stronger business case while our commercials are unresolved and scope has been cut.

competitors.name: Unnamed peer S2P vendors (2, in ITTStc 3-vendor evaluation)
competitors.quote: ITTStc approved we continue the P2P evaluation based on 3 vendors with a S2P focus. Vendor selection end of April and recommendation/approval end of May/beg June
competitors.how_we_win: Get the phased standalone S2P pricing (Zycus-delivered, no partner) agreed and force a named-competitor disclosure; defend the Merlin agentic wedge with a quantified ROI model before the board decision.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics (future ERP path)
competitors.quote: I would be surprised if we don't go Microsoft way, but of course there needs to be a decision
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with proven dual-ERP support (auto-routed line items, separate POs per legal entity) so a Dynamics migration is a non-issue; pre-empt any Dynamics-native peer on AI maturity.

competitors.name: SAP ECC + Bear Shop (incumbent ERP/procurement)
competitors.quote: it has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions; this application will be decommissioned next year
competitors.how_we_win: Replace the failing Bear Shop free-text flow with catalog control and spend visibility; the decommission timeline forces a 2026 decision.

competitors.name: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository)
competitors.quote: 10,000 contracts stored in a SharePoint somewhere, and it was a little bit difficult to manage
competitors.how_we_win: AI bulk migration with metadata extraction and clause-based search displaces manual SharePoint management.

competitors.name: Adobe Sign (incumbent eSignature)
competitors.quote: We are using Adobe Sign today... does that mean we can remove our license with Adobe Sign and utilize your capabilities?
competitors.how_we_win: Offer Certinal as a priced optional line so B&O can compare against retaining Adobe Sign; integrate either way to remove adoption friction.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the criteria the buyer weighted most (AI maturity, dual-ERP support, phased commercials), with change management and Outlook email-capture compliance the exposed edges.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a single-threaded, board-gated, scope-cut evaluation where value beats price.
flags: Arm Jonas for the board: co-build the business case and pre-sell the IT & Digital Board / CFO before vendor selection - he owns the recommendation but has no second senior advocate.
Quantify the ROI (free-text spend leakage, contract cycle time, legal review hours) so a scope-cut $70k phase reads as value, not cost, against two unnamed peers who may undercut.
Multi-thread into finance and transformation: engage Mikkel Staun Joergensen (Head of Treasury and Tax) and re-engage Nickolaj Almskou Holler to de-risk single-threading on Jonas.

champion_strength.summary: Jonas Elber is a strong, active champion who owns the CLM/S2P recommendation, drove the no-RFP direct evaluation, rated Zycus ahead on AI, and was promoted to Head of Technology on 29 May 2026 while preparing the board business case. The gap is a second senior advocate at the board/CFO level and a finance sponsor.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strongly positioned: the Merlin agentic story (NegoGenie redline resolution, AP Smartdesk invoice extraction, Contract Risk Monitor, gen-AI executive summaries) was demoed in depth and Jonas rated Zycus ahead of competition on AI, grilling us on LLM architecture and hallucination controls. AI is our primary wedge; keep it the Phase-2 centerpiece.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 7 / Moderate, driven by Merlin in scope alongside iContract and Jonas's named AI interest. The calls arguably under-rate the appetite: Jonas interrogated the LLM stack, multi-LLM orchestration and hallucination controls (25 Nov 2025), signalling a genuinely AI-hungry buyer.

items.source: Avoma CLM demo part 2 2025-11-25; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-16

Single-threaded on Jonas; no second senior advocate at the board and no finance sponsor engaged.
Commercials unresolved and ROI unquantified while two unnamed S2P peers remain in the 3-vendor evaluation and could undercut.
Buyer-stated adoption risk, especially in legal where Kristine prefers manual Word workflows.

items.expected_effect: Restarts the stalled commercial thread and arms Jonas with a value case the board decides on, not price.

critical_signals.text: We are one of three vendors in a formal board-gated S2P evaluation and Jonas rated us ahead on AI, but two peers remain unnamed and could undercut while our pricing is unresolved.
The scope was cut from $138k to a $70k phased Phase 1 and the ROI is still unquantified, so the win now hinges on proving value over price.
The last genuine exchange was Jonas acknowledging the phased proposal on 15 May and going quiet for ~7 weeks as finance drowns in year-end close.
Finance now gates the deal: Mikkel Staun Joergensen, Head of Treasury and Tax, is the budget path but has never been engaged.
Merlin's agentic capabilities landed strongly with Jonas, keeping our AI differentiation the primary reason we are in a good position.

customer_preference.evidence: Jonas rated Zycus 'quite far compared to competition' on AI (25 Nov 2025) and asked for Zycus-specific commercials, but no decisive recent lean and the scope was cut into a phased phase.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (free-text spend blindness, 10k SharePoint contracts, finance-procurement disconnect) but no cost, cycle-time or savings figure quantified.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities 120d; field history 365d; contact roles full
gaps: No 2026 buyer call captured with a transcript; the 2026 read rests on the Clari email/next-step trail.
Two of the three S2P finalists remain unnamed.
CFO / economic buyer never named or engaged; finance (Mikkel Staun Joergensen) not yet met.
